title: Integrated Education Act
evidence: Integrated Education Act 2022 passed, placing a statutory duty on the Department of Education to encourage and facilitate integrated education.
note: Integrated Education Act (Northern Ireland) 2022. Replaces the old link to ukpga/2022/15, which is the Building Safety Act 2022.

title: Standalone Hate Crime Bill
evidence: Alliance Justice Minister Naomi Long dropped the Hate Crime Bill during the mandate — a self-inflicted miss on their own manifesto pledge.
note: The Hate Crime Bill was dropped during the mandate, so there is no Act to link; pointed at the Department of Justice policing and community safety section.

title: End academic selection at age 11
evidence: A Single Transfer Test was introduced in November 2024, entrenching rather than ending academic selection.
note: The Single Transfer Test (November 2024) is run by SEAG; no canonical Department of Education publication URL could be confirmed.

title: Single Equality Act
evidence: Progressing via the Tennyson Equality Bill and the Victims and Witnesses Bill; not yet enacted as of 2026.
note: Evidence references a 'Tennyson Equality Bill' and a 'Victims and Witnesses Bill'; neither could be verified to exist under those names. Flagged for review.

title: Ban conversion therapy
evidence: Alliance progressing a Private Member's Bill after the DUP Communities Minister stalled on legislation; a successful Assembly motion passed in June 2024.
note: Conversion practices motion debated 4 June 2024, passed 41-25.

title: Implement Bengoa health service reforms
evidence: Bengoa framework being implemented across HSC Trusts; reforms are ongoing but incomplete and not Alliance-led legislation.
note: Bengoa-driven 'Health and Wellbeing 2026: Delivering Together' framework.

title: Social housing retrofitting programme
evidence: Alliance supported the Programme for Government housing strategy including energy-efficiency retrofitting; not a standalone Alliance-led bill.
note: Housing Supply Strategy 2024-2039 (includes retrofitting / energy efficiency commitments).

title: Skills Strategy and Lifelong Learning Guarantee
evidence: Skills Strategy overlaps with the Economy Department's plans and is progressing; not an Alliance-originated piece of legislation.
note: Department for the Economy Skills Strategy ('Skills for a 10x Economy').

title: Childcare Subsidy Scheme
evidence: The NI Childcare Subsidy Scheme delivered approximately £8 million in savings for families while the DUP held the Education ministry.
note: Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme savings for working families.

title: 28 new schools announced
evidence: DUP Education Minister announced 28 new school build projects during their period in office.
note: The '28 new schools' announcement could not be pinned to a single Department of Education news URL.

title: Retain academic selection
evidence: Academic selection was retained and formalised through the Single Transfer Test introduced in November 2024.
note: Retention of academic selection via the Single Transfer Test; no canonical Department of Education publication URL confirmed.

title: 100% full-fibre broadband rollout
evidence: Northern Ireland is ahead of the rest of the UK in full-fibre broadband coverage, meeting the DUP's connectivity target.
note: Project Stratum full-fibre / NGA broadband rollout.

title: Fair funding formula for schools
evidence: The fair funding formula for schools is under ongoing discussion; no final settlement has been reached.
note: The fair-funding-formula discussion has no settled publication to link.

title: Energy efficiency retrofitting and Decent Homes standard
evidence: A Decent Homes consultation was launched, but social home starts collapsed to approximately 400 per year during the mandate.
note: Housing Supply Strategy 2024-2039 (energy efficiency / Decent Homes commitments sit within it).

title: Create 20,000 new jobs and expand apprenticeships
evidence: Economic development plans progressing under the Economy Department; this is no longer a DUP-held ministerial brief.
note: The 20,000-jobs / apprenticeships pledge has no single document; Department for the Economy topics section.

title: Full removal of the NI Protocol
evidence: The Windsor Framework (February 2023) eased Irish Sea border arrangements but did not remove them; the Protocol's fundamental structure remains in place.
note: The Windsor Framework. Already a correct deep link; left unchanged.

title: Rent controls, Rental Board and ban on no-fault evictions
evidence: Carroll's People's Housing Bill launched for public consultation in October 2024 and debated at Second Stage in February 2025; a 10% rent reduction amendment was tabled before the 2022 election but voted down.
note: 'New deal for private renters' debate, 24 February 2025 - the sitting around the People's Housing Bill Second Stage debate.

title: Retain the NI Housing Executive in public ownership
evidence: NIHE remains a public body; no reclassification or privatisation was pursued during the mandate.
note: NIHE remains in public ownership; NIHE corporate page.

title: Mass expansion of public housing on public land
evidence: NIHE borrowing restrictions have not been lifted; Carroll notes at current build rates it would take over 50 years to clear the 47,936-household waiting list.
note: NIHE borrowing restrictions not lifted; Communities housing section.

title: Trade Union Freedom Bill
evidence: Carroll's bill was voted down 43-38 in March 2022 (DUP, UUP and Alliance opposed; Sinn Féin and SDLP supported); it has not been re-introduced in the current mandate.
note: Trade Union Freedom Bill voted down March 2022 - exact sitting not pinned.

title: Inflation-busting pay rises for all public sector workers
evidence: Carroll backed strike action across health, education and civil service; the February 2024 Executive deal delivered pay parity with England (5% plus £1,505) but fell short of inflation-busting.
note: Public-sector pay / strike action across the mandate - no single sitting; Official Report index.

title: Local commissioning of abortion services
evidence: The Secretary of State commissioned services on 2 December 2022; services rolled out across all five HSC Trusts during 2023, with 2,899 procedures performed in 2024-25.
note: Secretary of State's December 2022 instruction to commission abortion services.

title: Oppose Translink privatisation
evidence: Translink remains fully in public ownership; no privatisation was proposed or pursued during the mandate.
note: Translink not privatised; Department for Infrastructure public transport section.

title: Abolish tuition fees
evidence: Tuition fees in NI remain approximately £4,750 per year for 2025-26; no bill was tabled to abolish them at Stormont.
note: Tuition fees not abolished; Department for the Economy higher education section.

title: Carbon neutrality for NI by 2035
evidence: The Climate Change Act (NI) 2022 sets net zero by 2050, not 2035; PBP's stronger amendments did not succeed.
note: Climate Change Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 - net zero by 2050, not 2035.

title: £1,000 emergency hardship payment for lower-income households
evidence: Stormont delivered a one-off £200 payment for benefit recipients; the universal £1,000 demand was not met.
note: £1,000 hardship payment not delivered; Communities benefits section.

title: Double social housing investment
evidence: Pushed through the 2024 Plan for Change targeting 13,000 new social homes by 2030, but the Executive is missing its own 2,500-homes-a-year target.
note: Housing Supply Strategy 2024-2039 - social-housing supply target.

title: Rent controls in the private sector
evidence: Mark H. Durkan tabled an Assembly motion in January 2026 calling for rent controls and a pause on the right-to-buy scheme.
note: Mark H. Durkan's January 2026 rent-controls motion - exact sitting date not pinned.

title: Mortgage Rescue Scheme and Empty Homes Strategy
evidence: No significant Assembly action has been identified on either policy during the mandate.
note: Mortgage Rescue Scheme / Empty Homes Strategy - no Assembly action identified; Communities housing section.

title: Magee University expansion to 10,000 students
evidence: SDLP MPs helped secure Westminster funding; the Magee Taskforce was launched in March 2024 and student numbers rose 22% to over 6,500.
note: Ulster University Magee Taskforce launch (March 2024).

title: 30 hours free pre-school childcare
evidence: Goal softened to halving childcare costs by 2030; SDLP helped secure the NI Childcare Subsidy Scheme and pushed for a draft Childcare Strategy in January 2025.
note: Northern Ireland Childcare Subsidy Scheme - the SDLP-backed childcare cost reduction.

title: Phase out academic selection at age 11
evidence: Position maintained in opposition, but no Assembly bill was tabled to abolish the transfer test during the mandate.
note: No Assembly bill tabled to abolish the transfer test; education topics.

title: 6% pay rise for nursing staff
evidence: SDLP passed a public-sector pay Assembly motion in March 2024; nurses were offered 5.5% for 2024-25, close to but short of the 6% target.
note: March 2024 public-sector pay motion - exact sitting date not pinned.

title: Ban conversion therapy
evidence: Colin McGrath moved a successful Assembly motion in June 2024, passed 41-25; legislation stalled by the DUP Communities Minister.
note: Conversion practices motion debated 4 June 2024, passed 41-25.

title: Irish Language Act
evidence: Identity and Language (NI) Act 2022 passed at Westminster in December 2022; SDLP MPs threatened amendments to force the issue through.
note: Identity and Language (Northern Ireland) Act 2022 (c. 45). Already a correct deep link; left unchanged.

title: Independent Environmental Protection Agency for NI
evidence: Patsy McGlone tabled a successful Assembly motion in May 2024 declaring an ecological crisis at Lough Neagh; NI remains the only part of the UK or Ireland without an independent EPA.
note: Independent Environmental Protection Agency / Lough Neagh ecological crisis debate, 13 May 2024.

title: New economic strategy maximising EU and GB market access
evidence: The economic strategy was delivered and adopted by the Executive as a core framework for the mandate, with four priorities (good jobs, regional balance, productivity, decarbonisation).
note: Economy Minister's economic vision / Economic Mission (four priorities).

title: Irish Language Commissioner appointment
evidence: Identity and Language (NI) Act 2022 in force; the first Irish Language Commissioner was appointed in November 2025.
note: Identity and Language (Northern Ireland) Act 2022 (c. 45) - establishes the Irish Language Commissioner. This chapter number is correct; the task brief's claim that ukpga/2022/45 is the Energy Prices Act 2022 is mistaken (that Act is c. 44). Left unchanged.

title: Commission reproductive healthcare services locally
evidence: Abortion services rolled out across all five HSC Trusts during 2023 following Westminster direction on 2 December 2022; 2,899 procedures were performed in 2024-25.
note: Secretary of State's December 2022 instruction to commission abortion services in Northern Ireland.

title: Violence Against Women and Girls Strategy
evidence: A VAWG Strategy was embedded in the Programme for Government as a core priority of the restored Executive.
note: Ending Violence Against Women and Girls strategic framework and delivery plans (The Executive Office).

title: Deliver 100,000 new homes over 15 years
evidence: The Executive adopted a matching target in December 2024, but delivery is behind schedule with a £128m social housing shortfall.
note: Housing Supply Strategy 2024-2039 - the Executive's 100,000-homes target adopted December 2024.

title: £1 billion health funding increase over three years
evidence: £215m has been ringfenced for waiting lists; the commitment is partially honoured but the full £1bn has not been allocated.
note: The '£1bn over three years' health pledge has no single document.

title: Tackle NHS waiting lists
evidence: The PfG waiting-list target was exceeded threefold on some measures, but over one in four of the NI population remain on a waiting list.
note: Waiting-list reduction; no canonical Elective Care Framework URL could be confirmed (the obvious slug 404s).

title: Magee University expansion
evidence: The Magee Taskforce was established; student numbers are up 22% to over 6,500, but the 10,000-student target remains distant.
note: Ulster University Magee Taskforce launch (March 2024).

title: End academic selection at age 11
evidence: The Single Transfer Test was introduced in November 2024, entrenching rather than ending academic selection — the opposite of the manifesto pledge.
note: Academic selection entrenched via the Single Transfer Test; no canonical Department of Education publication URL confirmed.

title: Fully funded Mental Health Strategy
evidence: Only 19 of 35 Mental Health Strategy actions have been started; the strategy remains chronically underfunded.
note: Mental Health Strategy 2021-2031.

title: Ban conversion therapy
evidence: No legislation has passed; the DUP Communities Minister has blocked progress throughout the mandate.
note: Conversion practices motion debated 4 June 2024 (Communities Minister declined to legislate).

title: Bill of Rights for Northern Ireland
evidence: No bill was introduced during the mandate; no cross-party consensus was achieved.
note: No Bill of Rights was introduced; Assembly legislation section.

title: Full removal of the NI Protocol and Windsor Framework
evidence: The Windsor Framework (February 2023) eased Irish Sea border checks but did not remove the Protocol's fundamental structure; TUV's primary electoral demand was not delivered.
note: The Windsor Framework. Already a correct deep link; left unchanged.

title: Restore NI's full place in the UK internal market
evidence: Northern Ireland continues to follow EU single-market rules in goods under the Windsor Framework; TUV's demand for full UK internal market access was not met.
note: The Windsor Framework. Already a correct deep link; left unchanged.

title: Retain academic selection and the grammar school system
evidence: Academic selection was retained; the Single Transfer Test introduced in November 2024 entrenches the selective system TUV supports.
note: Academic selection retained via the Single Transfer Test; education topics section.

title: Oppose expansion of abortion services in NI
evidence: The Secretary of State commissioned services on 2 December 2022; services are fully operational across all five HSC Trusts, contrary to TUV's position.
note: Secretary of State's December 2022 instruction to commission abortion services.

title: Oppose gender self-identification legislation
evidence: No self-identification legislation for gender identity has passed in NI; the UK Supreme Court's April 2025 ruling reinforced the biological definition under the Equality Act.
note: For Women Scotland Ltd v The Scottish Ministers [2025] UKSC 16 - 'sex' in the Equality Act 2010 means biological sex.

title: Strengthen law and order and oppose lenient sentencing
evidence: Justice system reforms progressing under the Alliance Justice Minister; sentencing guidelines under periodic review, though not driven by TUV legislation.
note: Law and order / sentencing - no TUV legislation; Department of Justice policing and community safety section.

title: Reduce the rates burden on small businesses
evidence: Rates relief extensions have been secured with cross-party support during the mandate; no TUV-specific legislation passed.
note: Business rates relief - no TUV legislation; Department of Finance property rating section.

title: Oppose deepening of cross-border bodies and all-island integration
evidence: Cross-border cooperation has continued and deepened under the restored Executive; all-island health and economic frameworks have progressed.
note: Cross-border cooperation deepened; North/South Ministerial Council site.

title: Cancer services strategy and rapid diagnostic centres
evidence: The first NI Cancer Research Strategic Framework was published and rapid diagnostic centres became operational.
note: A Cancer Strategy for Northern Ireland 2022-2032. The evidence string names a 'Cancer Research Strategic Framework', which is a separate, smaller document; flagged as a minor mismatch.

title: Dáithí's Law — opt-out organ donation
evidence: Dáithí's Law passed, introducing an opt-out system for organ donation in Northern Ireland.
note: Organ and Tissue Donation (Deemed Consent) Act (Northern Ireland) 2022 - 'Dáithí's Law'.

title: Housing as a standalone Programme for Government priority
evidence: Housing was established as a standalone PfG priority, securing dedicated focus and ringfenced funding discussions.
note: Programme for Government 2024-2027 'Our Plan: Doing What Matters Most' - housing as a standalone priority.

title: City Deals and regional Growth Deals
evidence: All City Deals progressing with cross-party support; significant investment secured across NI regions.
note: City and Growth Deals (Department for the Economy).

title: Reduce NHS waiting lists
evidence: A 24% drop in four-year-plus waits and a 58% drop in endoscopy waits, but 35% of NI patients still wait over two years versus single digits in England.
note: Waiting-list reduction; no canonical Elective Care Framework URL confirmed.

title: Stabilise the healthcare workforce
evidence: February 2024 pay deal delivered and agency spend reduced, but staffing pressures continue into 2025-26.
note: Healthcare workforce stabilisation; no single document.

title: Mental health funding and CAMHS investment
evidence: A Mental Health Delivery Plan was published but the strategy remains underfunded relative to need.
note: Mental Health Strategy 2021-2031 (CAMHS / mental health funding sits within it).

title: Reform NIHE to raise private finance for housing
evidence: Reform discussions are ongoing; no legislative change has been made to NIHE's borrowing powers.
note: NIHE borrowing reform - no legislative change made; Communities housing section.

title: Single multi-denominational education system
evidence: No movement toward a single system; denominational schooling remains the norm and no legislation was introduced.
note: No move toward a single multi-denominational system; education topics.

title: Reduce corporation tax to 15%
evidence: Corporation tax devolution has not been secured; the power remains with Westminster and no formal transfer was agreed.
note: Corporation tax devolution was not secured; economy strategy section.
